Rocket League is one of those games that's impossible to put down — I've sunk hundreds of hours into it and still find myself coming back, chasing that perfect aerial or that one clean redirect that makes everything click. The gameplay itself is genuinely brilliant: simple enough to pick up in minutes, but the ceiling on skill is basically infinite, and every goal you score feels earned through pure mechanics rather than luck.
removing trading, gutting the progression system, and turning what used to be a thriving community into a cash-grab storefront was a gut punch to longtime fans.
the core game is still one of the best ever made, and I'd recommend it to anyone who wants a competitive experience unlike anything else

nothing compares to the rush of a last second save or the high of a perfect goal and even the heartbreak of missing an open net is part of the endless joy that puts this game in my top five of all time despite me not even calling myself a gamer it’s just a decade of unmatched excitement that never ends
